jokes.text: His name's Gary, and we don't need any more lawsuits. No, I was talking about the magazine- Wait. Gary's gay?
jokes.setup: Michael assumes Gob is talking about a person named Poof
jokes.punchline: Gob was talking about Poof magazine, then realizes Gary is gay
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with tight dialogue and character-specific confusion

jokes.text: You've got a nice mouth. I'd kill for that ass.
jokes.setup: Gob worries Gary thinks he was coming on to him
jokes.punchline: We hear what sounds like flirtatious comments
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup that misdirects audience into thinking Gob was actually flirting

jokes.text: Okay, the chair's not doing it now... but lately it's been giving out as soon as I lean back.
jokes.setup: Previous lines sound sexual
jokes.punchline: Gob was talking about furniture problems the whole time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended misdirection that recontextualizes everything we just heard

jokes.text: I was gonna boil myself alive into a chowder and then be ladled into a giant cauldron... to entertain and feed the firemen.
jokes.setup: Gob claims he had Tony Wonder's bread trick idea first
jokes.punchline: His version involves becoming soup for firemen
jokes.explanation: Peak Gob absurdity - completely character-specific ridiculous logic

jokes.text: And I'm halfway to getting out of this office and into a steaming bowl of soup.
jokes.setup: Gob just mentioned his chowder trick
jokes.punchline: He's still thinking about literally becoming soup
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Gob is still obsessed with his soup idea

jokes.text: Oscar, close it. You look like the window of a butcher shop.
jokes.setup: Oscar is presumably underdressed or exposed
jokes.punchline: Lucille's cutting comparison to meat display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille insult - visual, cutting, and economical

jokes.text: See? That's a perfect example of how your father's always interrup- [Vibrating]
jokes.setup: Ann complains about Michael always interrupting
jokes.punchline: Michael's phone immediately interrupts her complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony - the interruption proves her point

jokes.text: I didn't wanna say that when you were talking to Egg.
jokes.setup: Michael is on phone with George Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael calls Ann 'Egg' instead of her name
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael dismissiveness shown through deliberate name confusion

jokes.text: Oh, she's still goin'?
jokes.setup: George Michael says he's still talking with Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael acts like Ann is an endurance test
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's casual cruelty about George Michael's girlfriend

jokes.text: I am surprised, though, that she's going after somebody so similar to my own type. But I suppose we all do expose our inner desires, don't we? I think you just did.
jokes.setup: Oscar comments on Lindsay flirting with Ice
jokes.punchline: Tobias accidentally reveals his own attraction to Oscar's type
jokes.explanation: Layered joke about sexual confusion that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: I'm sorry- he killed a guard? No, the guard killed him.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who died
jokes.punchline: Ice clarifies George Sr. was killed, not the killer
jokes.explanation: Simple confusion joke that adds to the chaos of the moment

jokes.text: Okay, again, I'm a caterer... so if you do have any family events- weddings, wakes- I'll leave my card.
jokes.setup: Ice just delivered news of George Sr.'s death
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to drum up catering business from the wake
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Ice's entrepreneurial spirit overrides social appropriateness

jokes.text: Who left the cap off my- [Bleeps] Glisten?
jokes.setup: Family discussing George Sr.'s perfect teeth
jokes.punchline: Lucille mimics his obsessive concern about toothpaste cap
jokes.explanation: Reveals George Sr.'s petty obsessions through Lucille's impression

jokes.text: I will be buried in my father's place... and then, one week later, I will emerge from the grave... in one of the greatest illusions ever! This about getting into Poof? I mean, how does that not get me a cover?
jokes.setup: Gob proposes elaborate burial illusion as tribute
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately calls out his real selfish motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - grandiose gesture with purely selfish motivation

jokes.text: Because you're twins. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Lucille claims it's George Sr.'s birthday, coincidentally Oscar's too
jokes.punchline: Buster states the obvious reason with innocent delight
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster naivety - he's delighted by basic facts

jokes.text: What? What? Is Ann telling everyone?
jokes.setup: George Michael says he has bad news
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately assumes it's about Ann gossiping
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's priorities and paranoia about Ann

jokes.text: For those other guys. You think there are other guys?
jokes.setup: Michael says Ann is great 'for those other guys'
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets jealous and worried about competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of Michael's inappropriate jealousy - he can't help but get possessive even while insulting Ann

jokes.text: For those other guys. You think there are other guys? I don't know what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to be supportive about breakup
jokes.punchline: He accidentally plants seeds of jealousy then backtracks
jokes.explanation: Michael's well-meaning but harmful parenting instincts

jokes.text: Black humor. Say, 'Hey, Dad, look at you. You're... a year older... and a year closer to death.' Oh, yeah, I guess that's kinda funny.
jokes.setup: Buster asks why coffin trick for birthday
jokes.punchline: Gob's explanation is genuinely dark, Buster finds it charming
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works because both characters misunderstand the appropriateness

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. I used to be a stripper. Strippers don't wear clothes. Not at the end of the show. You can wear stripper clothes when you're not stripping? You tell me.
jokes.setup: Buster needs military uniform from Gob's stripper past
jokes.punchline: Buster's innocent confusion about stripper clothing logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster logic trying to understand adult concepts

jokes.text: Hide me. Don't turn in Pop-Pop. Help Pop-Pop.
jokes.setup: George Michael discovers his grandfather alive
jokes.punchline: George Sr. immediately manipulates using childish nickname
jokes.explanation: Shows George Sr.'s manipulative nature using family bonds

jokes.text: I have, uh- I have money.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains George Sr. found loophole in Mexican system
jokes.punchline: The 'loophole' is just bribing officials
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag - the anticlimax of calling basic corruption a 'loophole' is perfectly cynical

jokes.text: Listen, if you pass a mini-mart, Pop-Pop gets a treat?
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to bond with George Michael
jokes.punchline: He refers to himself in third person like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George Sr.'s pathetic manipulation tactics

jokes.text: I was just gonna eat some of my grandfather's favorite foods in my room. It's the form that my grief is taking.
jokes.setup: Michael catches George Michael with food
jokes.punchline: George Michael's elaborate lie about grief eating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvisation that sounds psychological and plausible

jokes.text: From who, the Nazis?
jokes.setup: Michael thinks George Michael is hiding Ann in attic
jokes.punchline: Gob makes Anne Frank reference
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference that perfectly fits the hiding scenario

jokes.text: I tried it with Mom's, but I'd just end up smiling and ruining it.
jokes.setup: Gob explains how he used to fake cry using Dad's death
jokes.punchline: Thinking about Mom's death makes him happy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark character moment revealing Gob's relationship with his mother

jokes.text: Here comes John Wayne. I'm not gonna cry about my pa. I'm gonna build an airport, put my name on it.
jokes.setup: Gob says Michael bottles up emotions
jokes.punchline: Michael's overly macho response proves Gob's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-parody of masculine emotional suppression

jokes.text: Hey, where the- [Bleeps] Are my hard-boiled eggs?
jokes.setup: Michael gives speech about not bottling emotions
jokes.punchline: Immediately explodes over missing eggs
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction that proves Gob's point about emotional suppression

jokes.text: 'Someone wanted to send you a birthday greeting. See you soon!' Oh! That is wicked.
jokes.setup: Buster shopping for father's birthday card
jokes.punchline: He picks ominous card perfect for fake death scenario
jokes.explanation: Innocent Buster unknowingly picking the most inappropriate card

jokes.text: I'm gonna throw on a skirt, take off my underwear and make your Pop-Pop proud.
jokes.setup: Maeby encourages Lindsay to seduce Ice
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's overly explicit plan of seduction
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's inappropriate oversharing about seduction tactics

jokes.text: Her? She's really funny. Well, let's hope so.
jokes.setup: George Michael points out Ann at the wake
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive assessment of her looks vs. personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive put-down disguised as optimism

jokes.text: It wasn't a message. We talked.
jokes.setup: Michael says he left Ann a message to invite her
jokes.punchline: Ann corrects that they had an actual conversation he forgot
jokes.explanation: Subtle character beat - shows Michael finds Ann so forgettable he doesn't remember talking to her

jokes.text: It's as Ann as the nose on Plain's face.
jokes.setup: Michael talks about George Michael and Ann caring for each other
jokes.punchline: Mangled idiom replacing 'plain' with Ann and 'Ann' with plain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that insults Ann while pretending to be supportive - peak AD writing

jokes.text: It's as Ann as the nose on Plain's face.
jokes.setup: Michael saying a father can tell George Michael cares about Ann
jokes.punchline: Mangles 'plain as the nose on your face' to insult Ann twice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - manages to mangle a common idiom while delivering two insults (Ann/plain) in one phrase. Peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: To get the top right, the pants had to be a little snug.
jokes.setup: Buster arrives in military stripper outfit
jokes.punchline: Visual of inappropriate tight pants explained matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to earlier stripper costume setup

jokes.text: How many times I gotta tell this kid chicken wings?
jokes.setup: George Michael mentions salmon rolls in eulogy
jokes.punchline: Hidden George Sr. corrects him about his food preferences
jokes.explanation: Shows George Sr.'s pettiness even during his own fake eulogy

jokes.text: Glad Dad wasn't around to see that.
jokes.setup: Gob criticizes Michael's short eulogy
jokes.punchline: Ironic since they're at Dad's fake funeral
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony since audience knows George Sr. is alive and listening

jokes.text: The mere fact that you call making love 'pop-pop' tells me you're not ready.
jokes.setup: George Michael says 'I have Pop-Pop in the attic'
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks he's talking about sex using childish term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that becomes instant classic quote

jokes.text: The mere fact that you call making love 'pop-pop' tells me you're not ready.
jokes.setup: George Michael says he has 'Pop-Pop in the attic'
jokes.punchline: Michael misunderstands and thinks he's talking about sex
jokes.explanation: Exceptional misdirection - the misunderstanding is perfectly logical and creates one of the show's most quotable lines. Michael's fatherly concern about sexual readiness is hilariously misplaced.

jokes.text: Dead father? Is he de- Dad's dead? Oh, my God! The card! The card!
jokes.setup: Gob mentions 'dead father' during magic trick
jokes.punchline: Buster realizes the truth and panics about his 'wicked' card choice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff - Buster's horror at his accidentally inappropriate card choice creates perfect chaos during the magic trick

jokes.text: I can't seem to give this away.
jokes.setup: Barry finds the will after chaos has settled
jokes.punchline: No one cares about the will anymore
jokes.explanation: Barry's oblivious timing perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: The great thing is, she was so moved by what you said tonight that we're back together. Ah! Yeah! Oh, is that great.
jokes.setup: George Michael reveals he's back with Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael's fake enthusiasm barely concealing horror
jokes.explanation: Michael's barely contained dismay at his plan backfiring

jokes.text: [Preview: Buster finds out about Captain Kangaroo's death]
jokes.setup: Preview of next episode
jokes.punchline: Buster will react to another death with same trauma
jokes.explanation: Sets up continuation of Buster's psychological patterns

jokes.text: No, Pop-Pop does not get a treat. I just brought you a- [Bleeps] pizza.
jokes.setup: Preview showing Michael caring for hidden George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperation with George Sr.'s childish demands
jokes.explanation: Shows how George Sr.'s manipulation will continue to frustrate Michael

jokes.text: No, Pop-Pop does not get a treat. I just brought you a- [Bleeps] pizza.
jokes.setup: Michael caring for hidden George Sr. in preview
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustration with his father's demands, echoing earlier hard-boiled eggs outburst
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Michael's pattern of emotional outbursts over food, now directed at his demanding father
